item.title: [Solution] macOS Network Setup Error — Network Interfaces Not Configured
item.description:
Network Setup Error — Network Interfaces Not Configured

Fix macOS Network Setup error: network interfaces not configured, network locations broken, network preferences corrupted.

Common Causes

  • Network location settings corrupted
  • Multiple network configurations conflicting
  • System network preferences file corrupted
  • Third-party network utility modifying system settings

How to Fix

1. Check Network Interfaces

networksetup -listallhardwareports
ifconfig -a
networksetup -listallnetworkservices

2. Create New Network Location

# System Settings → Network → Locations → Edit Locations → Add → Create new location

3. Reset Network Preferences

sudo rm -f /Library/Preferences/SystemConfiguration/NetworkInterfaces.plist
sudo rm -f /Library/Preferences/SystemConfiguration/preferences.plist
sudo shutdown -r now

4. Repair Network from Recovery

# Recovery → Reinstall macOS to restore network configuration files
# This preserves data but resets network settings

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Store Error — App Store Not Loading
item.description:
App Store Error — App Store Not Loading

Fix macOS App Store error: App Store not loading, App Store blank page, App Store connection error or crash.

Common Causes

  • App Store cache corrupted or outdated
  • Apple ID authentication issue with App Store
  • Network connection preventing App Store from loading
  • App Store application bundle corrupted

How to Fix

1. Clear App Store Cache

rm -rf ~/Library/Caches/com.apple.appstore
rm -rf ~/Library/Cookies/com.apple.appstore*
killall App Store

2. Sign Out and Back Into Apple ID

# App Store → Store → Sign Out → Sign In
# Or System Settings → Apple ID → Sign Out → Sign In

3. Reset App Store Preferences

defaults delete com.apple.appstore
rm -rf ~/Library/Preferences/com.apple.appstore.plist
sudo shutdown -r now

4. Check Apple System Status

# Visit https://www.apple.com/support/systemstatus/ for App Store outages

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Store Download Error — Apps Stuck Downloading
item.description:
App Store Download Error — Apps Stuck Downloading

Fix macOS App Store download failure: apps stuck on downloading, App Store download interrupted or spinning indefinitely.

Common Causes

  • App Store download cache corrupted
  • Insufficient disk space for app download and installation
  • Network interruption during app download
  • Apple ID authentication token expired during download

How to Fix

1. Clear App Store Download Cache

rm -rf ~/Library/Caches/com.apple.appstore
rm -rf ~/Library/Updates/*
killall 'App Store'

2. Check Disk Space

df -h /
# Ensure at least 2x app size free for download and installation

3. Reset Download and Try Again

# App Store → Account → find paused download → click Resume or Cancel and retry

4. Sign Out and Back Into Apple ID

# App Store → Store → Sign Out
# Wait 30 seconds → Sign In → Retry download

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Store Update Error — Updates Not Appearing
item.description:
App Store Update Error — Updates Not Appearing

Fix macOS App Store update failure: updates not appearing, update stuck downloading, App Store update error message.

Common Causes

  • App Store not checking for updates correctly
  • Update server connection issue
  • App Store update cache not refreshing
  • macOS update notification system not triggering

How to Fix

1. Check for Updates Manually

softwareupdate -l
# Or open App Store → Updates tab

2. Force App Store to Refresh

# App Store → Store → Reload Page (Command+R)
rm -rf ~/Library/Caches/com.apple.appstore
killall 'App Store'

3. Reset App Store Update Preferences

defaults delete com.apple.appstore
defaults write com.apple.appstore ShowUpdateStatusBar -bool true
killall 'App Store'

4. Install Updates via Terminal

softwareupdate -i -a
# This installs all available updates bypassing App Store UI

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Store Purchase Error — Cannot Buy Apps
item.description:
App Store Purchase Error — Cannot Buy Apps

Fix macOS App Store purchase failure: cannot buy apps, payment declined, purchase verification loop, purchase not completing.

Common Causes

  • Payment method expired or invalid
  • Apple ID payment information needs updating
  • Purchase verification failing due to authentication issue
  • Region or country mismatch between Apple ID and payment method

How to Fix

1. Update Payment Information

# System Settings → Apple ID → Payment & Shipping → Update payment method
# Ensure card is valid and not expired

2. Verify Apple ID Account

# System Settings → Apple ID → Payment & Shipping → Verify billing address

3. Try Different Payment Method

# Add different credit card or use Apple Pay for purchase

4. Contact Bank for Authorization

# Call bank to authorize App Store purchases on your card

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Store Refund Error — Cannot Request Refund
item.description:
App Store Refund Error — Cannot Request Refund

Fix macOS App Store refund issue: cannot request refund, refund not appearing, App Store refund denied or delayed.

Common Causes

  • Refund request submitted outside allowed time window
  • App Store refund policy does not cover the purchase type
  • Refund request denied by Apple due to usage evidence
  • Payment method cannot receive refunds

How to Fix

1. Request Refund via Report a Problem

open https://reportaproblem.apple.com
# Sign in with Apple ID → Find purchase → Request Refund

2. Check Refund Status

# reportaproblem.apple.com → Check status of previous refund requests

3. Contact Apple Support

# Visit https://support.apple.com/contact → Request refund assistance

4. Check Payment Method for Refund

# Check card statement for refund credit after approval

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Store ID Error — Apple ID Not Valid
item.description:
App Store ID Error — Apple ID Not Valid

Fix macOS Apple ID error in App Store: Apple ID not valid, cannot sign in to App Store, ID verification failed.

Common Causes

  • Apple ID locked due to too many failed login attempts
  • Two-factor authentication code expired or incorrect
  • Apple ID associated with different region App Store
  • Apple ID password needs to be reset

How to Fix

1. Sign In With Correct Apple ID

# System Settings → Apple ID → Verify you're using correct Apple ID
# Check for multiple Apple IDs: defaults read MobileMeAccounts

2. Reset Apple ID Password

# Visit https://iforgot.apple.com to reset password

3. Unlock Apple ID

# Visit https://iforgot.apple.com → Unlock Apple ID account

4. Check Two-Factor Authentication

# Ensure 2FA code from trusted device is entered within 30 seconds

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Store Region Error — Wrong Store Region
item.description:
App Store Region Error — Wrong Store Region

Fix macOS App Store region error: App Store shows wrong region, cannot change country, region-locked content not accessible.

Common Causes

  • Apple ID region set to wrong country
  • App Store region changed automatically based on IP
  • Region-locked content only available in specific countries
  • Country/region change requires valid payment method for new region

How to Fix

1. Check Apple ID Region

# System Settings → Apple ID → Media & Purchases → View Account → Country/Region

2. Change Country/Region

# System Settings → Apple ID → Media & Purchases → View Account → Country/Region → Change

3. Create New Apple ID for Different Region

# Visit https://appleid.apple.com → Create new Apple ID with target region

4. Use Gift Card for Different Region

# Purchase Apple Gift Card for target region → Redeem in App Store

Common Scenarios

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Store Payment Error — Payment Method Rejected
item.description:
App Store Payment Error — Payment Method Rejected

Fix macOS App Store payment failure: payment method rejected, cannot add payment, billing information error, card declined.

Common Causes

  • Credit or debit card expired or has insufficient funds
  • Bank blocking international or digital transaction
  • Billing address does not match card issuer records
  • Apple payment system temporarily unavailable

How to Fix

1. Update Payment Method

# System Settings → Apple ID → Payment & Shipping → Edit or add payment method

2. Verify Billing Address

# System Settings → Apple ID → Payment & Shipping → Billing Address → Verify

3. Try Alternative Payment Method

# Use different card, Apple Pay, or Apple Gift Card balance

4. Contact Bank

# Call card issuer to authorize App Store / Apple transactions

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Store Family Sharing Error — Family Purchase Not Available
item.description:
App Store Family Sharing Error — Family Purchase Not Available

Fix macOS App Store Family Sharing error: family purchase not available, family organizer cannot share apps, sharing invite failed.

Common Causes

  • Family Sharing not properly configured
  • Family organizer Apple ID issue preventing purchases
  • Family member not properly added to family group
  • Purchase sharing not enabled in Family Sharing settings

How to Fix

1. Check Family Sharing Status

# System Settings → Apple ID → Family → Verify family members
# Check if Purchase Sharing is enabled

2. Enable Purchase Sharing

# System Settings → Apple ID → Family → Purchase Sharing → Enable

3. Re-add Family Member

# System Settings → Apple ID → Family → Remove member → Re-invite
# Ensure invitation is accepted from family member's device

4. Verify Payment Method

# System Settings → Apple ID → Payment & Shipping → Ensure valid payment method for organizer

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS Gatekeeper Error — App Blocked from Opening
item.description:
Gatekeeper Error — App Blocked from Opening

Fix macOS Gatekeeper error: app blocked by Gatekeeper, cannot open unidentified developer app, Gatekeeper settings prevent launch.

Common Causes

  • App not signed with Apple-issued developer certificate
  • Gatekeeper set to allow apps only from App Store
  • Developer ID certificate expired or revoked
  • App downloaded from untrusted source flagged by Gatekeeper

How to Fix

1. Override Gatekeeper for Specific App

# Right-click app → Open → Click Open in confirmation dialog
# Or: sudo xattr -rd com.apple.quarantine /path/to/app.app

2. Adjust Gatekeeper Settings

# System Settings → Privacy & Security → Security → Allow apps from: App Store and identified developers

3. Check App Signature

codesign -dv --verbose=4 /path/to/app.app
# Look for 'Signature=' and 'Authority=' in output

4. Temporarily Disable Gatekeeper (Not Recommended)

sudo spctl --master-disable
# Re-enable after installation: sudo spctl --master-enable

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS Notarization Error — App Not Notarized by Apple
item.description:
Notarization Error — App Not Notarized by Apple

Fix macOS notarization error: app cannot be opened because it has not been notarized, Apple notarization failed, app requires notarization.

Common Causes

  • App developer has not submitted app for Apple notarization
  • Apple notarization service rejected the app submission
  • App was built with outdated development tools
  • Notarization ticket not embedded in the app bundle

How to Fix

1. Bypass Notarization Check

# Right-click app → Open → Click Open in dialog
# Or: sudo xattr -rd com.apple.quarantine /path/to/app.app

2. Check Notarization Status

stapler validate /path/to/app.app
# Or: spctl -a -v /path/to/app.app

3. Contact App Developer

# Ask developer to submit app to Apple for notarization

4. Adjust Gatekeeper Settings

# System Settings → Privacy & Security → Allow apps from identified developers

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS Code Signing Error — App Signature Invalid
item.description:
Code Signing Error — App Signature Invalid

Fix macOS code signing error: app signature invalid, code signature verification failed, ad-hoc signing error, signature corrupt.

Common Causes

  • App code signature corrupted during download or transfer
  • Developer certificate used for signing has expired
  • Code signature does not match the app binary
  • Third-party modification invalidated original signature

How to Fix

1. Verify Code Signature

codesign -dv --verbose=4 /path/to/app.app
spctl -a -v /path/to/app.app

2. Re-sign App (Developer Only)

codesign -f -s 'Developer ID Application: Name' /path/to/app.app
# Requires valid Apple Developer certificate

3. Remove and Re-download App

# Delete current copy → Re-download from official source

4. Check Signature in Keychain Access

# Keychain Access → Certificates → Verify developer certificate is valid

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS Entitlement Error — App Missing Required Entitlements
item.description:
Entitlement Error — App Missing Required Entitlements

Fix macOS entitlement error: app missing required entitlements, sandbox entitlement violation, provisioning profile error.

Common Causes

  • App binary missing required entitlements for macOS features
  • Provisioning profile entitlements do not match app capabilities
  • Entitlement plist file corrupted or misconfigured
  • App requesting entitlements not allowed by macOS sandbox

How to Fix

1. Check App Entitlements

codesign -d --entitlements - /path/to/app.app
# List all entitlements in the app's code signature

2. Verify Provisioning Profile

# Xcode → Preferences → Accounts → Download profiles

3. Re-sign with Correct Entitlements

# Requires valid Apple Developer account and signing identity

4. Contact App Developer

# Developer needs to fix entitlements in Xcode before redistributing

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS Sandbox Restriction Error — App Cannot Access Files
item.description:
Sandbox Restriction Error — App Cannot Access Files

Fix macOS sandbox restriction error: app cannot access files outside sandbox, sandbox violation in Console, file access denied.

Common Causes

  • App sandbox profile restricting file system access
  • App requesting access to files outside its sandbox container
  • macOS sandbox enforcement blocking legacy file operations
  • App needs specific file access entitlement not granted

How to Fix

1. Check Sandbox Permissions

# System Settings → Privacy & Security → File and Folders → Enable for app

2. Grant Full Disk Access

# System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access → Add app

3. Check App Sandbox Profile

# App → Contents → _MASReceipt → Check sandbox entitlements

4. Contact Developer

# Developer needs to add required entitlements or adjust sandbox profile

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Translocation Error — App Moved to Quarantine
item.description:
App Translocation Error — App Moved to Quarantine

Fix macOS app translocation: app moved to quarantine location, app translocated and cannot save data, random path prefix.

Common Causes

  • macOS translocating app to prevent unsigned code execution
  • App opened from DMG or unverified source triggering translocation
  • Quarantine attribute causing path randomization
  • App data stored in translocated path lost after restart

How to Fix

1. Remove Quarantine Attribute

sudo xattr -rd com.apple.quarantine /path/to/app.app
# Remove quarantine flag to prevent translocation

2. Move App to Applications Folder

# Drag app from DMG directly to /Applications/ instead of opening from DMG

3. Check Translocation Path

ps aux | grep app
# Look for randomized /var/folders/ path in process info

4. Reinstall App from Trusted Source

# Delete translocated copy → Download fresh from official source

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Quarantine Error — App Downloaded from Internet Warning
item.description:
App Quarantine Error — App Downloaded from Internet Warning

Fix macOS app quarantine error: app downloaded from internet warning every launch, cannot remove quarantine extended attribute.

Common Causes

  • Quarantine extended attribute set during download
  • Browser or download manager setting quarantine flag on all downloads
  • Gatekeeper re-applying quarantine after removal
  • Quarantine database preventing app from running without warning

How to Fix

1. Remove Quarantine Attribute

xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /path/to/app.app
# Remove quarantine flag from downloaded app

2. Remove Quarantine Recursively

xattr -rd com.apple.quarantine /path/to/app.app
# Remove quarantine from app and all its contents

3. Check Quarantine Status

xattr -l /path/to/app.app
# Look for com.apple.quarantine in extended attributes

4. Download App from App Store

# Download from App Store instead of third-party website to avoid quarantine

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Crash Error — App Quits Unexpectedly
item.description:
App Crash Error — App Quits Unexpectedly

Fix macOS app crash: app quits unexpectedly, crash report generated, app crashes on launch or during use, crash log created.

Common Causes

  • App bug or code error causing unexpected termination
  • Incompatible app version with current macOS
  • Corrupted app preferences or cache files
  • System resource exhaustion causing app to be killed

How to Fix

1. Check Crash Reports

ls -lt ~/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports/ | head -10
cat ~/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports/AppName_YYYY-MM-DD.crash

2. Delete App Preferences

defaults delete com.developer.appname
rm -rf ~/Library/Caches/com.developer.appname

3. Reinstall the App

# Delete app from /Applications/
# Download fresh copy from App Store or developer website

4. Check System Resources

top -l 1 -o cpu -n 10
# Check if system is low on memory or CPU

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Freeze Error — Application Stops Responding
item.description:
App Freeze Error — Application Stops Responding

Fix macOS app freeze: application stops responding, beachball appears, app must be force quit to recover, UI completely locked.

Common Causes

  • App performing blocking operation on main thread
  • System running out of available memory causing swap thrashing
  • App waiting on network resource that is not responding
  • Infinite loop in app code consuming all CPU resources

How to Fix

1. Force Quit Frozen App

# Command+Option+Escape → Select app → Force Quit
# Or: killall -9 'App Name'

2. Check App Resource Usage

top -l 1 -o cpu -n 5 | grep 'App Name'
# Check CPU and memory usage of frozen app

3. Clear App Cache

rm -rf ~/Library/Caches/com.developer.appname
# Restart app after clearing cache

4. Monitor System Resources

memory_pressure
# Check if system is under memory pressure causing freezes

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Uninstall Error — Cannot Delete App
item.description:
App Uninstall Error — Cannot Delete App

Fix macOS app uninstall failure: cannot delete app, app leftover files, app still running after moving to trash, uninstall incomplete.

Common Causes

  • App still running in background preventing deletion
  • App installed with installer leaving background processes
  • System integrity protection preventing app removal
  • App has launch agents or daemons still active

How to Fix

1. Force Quit and Delete App

killall -9 'App Name'
# Move app to Trash: drag to Trash or Command+Delete

2. Remove App Leftover Files

rm -rf ~/Library/Preferences/com.developer.appname*
rm -rf ~/Library/Caches/com.developer.appname*
rm -rf ~/Library/Application\ Support/AppName

3. Check for Background Processes

ps aux | grep -i 'appname'
# Kill any remaining background processes

4. Remove Login Items and Launch Agents

# System Settings → General → Login Items → Remove app
# Remove from ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ if present

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Login Item Error — App Not Starting at Login
item.description:
App Login Item Error — App Not Starting at Login

Fix macOS login item error: app not starting at login, cannot add or remove login items, login items missing, startup delay.

Common Causes

  • Login item corrupted or improperly configured
  • Launch agent plist file missing executable reference
  • macOS blocking login items for security reasons
  • Login item conflicting with other startup processes

How to Fix

1. Check Login Items

# System Settings → General → Login Items → Review list
# osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to get name of every login item'

2. Add App as Login Item

# System Settings → General → Login Items → Add app via + button

3. Remove Problematic Login Items

# System Settings → General → Login Items → Select item → Remove via - button

4. Check Launch Agents

ls ~/Library/LaunchAgents/
ls /Library/LaunchAgents/
# Verify plist files point to valid executables

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Notification Error — Notifications Not Showing
item.description:
App Notification Error — Notifications Not Showing

Fix macOS app notification error: notifications not showing for app, notification badge stuck, notification settings lost.

Common Causes

  • App notification permissions disabled at system level
  • Focus mode blocking notifications from specific app
  • Notification settings corrupted after macOS update
  • App not properly registered with Notification Center

How to Fix

1. Check App Notification Settings

# System Settings → Notifications → Find app → Allow Notifications ON
# Verify alert style, sound, and badge settings

2. Reset Notification Preferences

defaults delete com.apple.notificationcenterui
rm -rf ~/Library/Caches/com.apple.notificationcenter*
sudo shutdown -r now

3. Restart Notification Center

killall notificationcenterd
killall usernoted

4. Test Notification

osascript -e 'display notification "Test" with title "Notification Test"'

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Accessibility Error — VoiceOver or Accessibility Not Working
item.description:
App Accessibility Error — VoiceOver or Accessibility Not Working

Fix macOS accessibility error: app not compatible with VoiceOver, accessibility permissions denied, accessibility API error.

Common Causes

  • App not implementing accessibility API properly
  • Accessibility permissions not granted to app
  • VoiceOver not detecting app UI elements
  • Third-party app conflicting with accessibility features

How to Fix

1. Grant Accessibility Permissions

# System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility → Enable for app

2. Check Accessibility API Status

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to get UI elements of (first process whose name is "AppName")'

3. Reset Accessibility Permissions

# System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility → Remove and re-add app

4. Test with VoiceOver

# Press Command+F5 to enable VoiceOver
# Navigate with VoiceOver keys to test app accessibility

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Keychain Error — App Cannot Access Keychain
item.description:
App Keychain Error — App Cannot Access Keychain

Fix macOS app keychain error: app cannot access keychain, keychain prompt not appearing, keychain item not found, password not saved.

Common Causes

  • Keychain access permissions not granted to app
  • Keychain item deleted or corrupted
  • App not properly configured for keychain access
  • Keychain locked preventing app from reading stored credentials

How to Fix

1. Check Keychain Access

security find-generic-password -s 'service-name' ~/Library/Keychains/login.keychain-db

2. Grant Keychain Access

# Keychain Access → Find item → Get Info → Access Control → Add app

3. Reset Keychain Access List

# Keychain Access → Login keychain → Access Control → Remove all → Add app

4. Unlock Keychain

security unlock-keychain ~/Library/Keychains/login.keychain-db
# Enter keychain password when prompted

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App iCloud Error — App Cannot Save to iCloud
item.description:
App iCloud Error — App Cannot Save to iCloud

Fix macOS app iCloud error: app cannot save to iCloud, iCloud document sync broken, iCloud storage full, app data lost.

Common Causes

  • iCloud Drive not enabled for the app
  • iCloud storage full preventing app from saving data
  • iCloud sync daemon not running or crashed
  • App iCloud container permissions misconfigured

How to Fix

1. Check iCloud Drive for App

# System Settings → Apple ID → iCloud → Apps Using iCloud → Enable for app

2. Check iCloud Storage

# System Settings → Apple ID → iCloud → Manage Account Storage
# Free up space or upgrade iCloud plan

3. Restart iCloud Sync

killall bird
# iCloud sync daemon will restart automatically

4. Force App to Re-sync

# App → File → Move to/Move from iCloud Drive → Re-sync documents

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Sandbox Error — Sandbox Profile Invalid
item.description:
App Sandbox Error — Sandbox Profile Invalid

Fix macOS app sandbox error: sandboxed app cannot access file system, sandbox profile invalid, sandbox escape issue reported.

Common Causes

  • App sandbox profile not properly configured
  • Sandbox entitlements not matching app capabilities
  • App trying to access resources outside sandbox container
  • macOS sandbox enforcement tightened after security update

How to Fix

1. Check App Sandbox Status

codesign -d --entitlements - /path/to/app.app | grep -i sandbox

2. Grant File System Access

# System Settings → Privacy & Security → Files and Folders → Enable for app

3. Review Sandbox Profile

# App → Contents → Check _MASReceipt and entitlements plist

4. Contact Developer

# Developer needs to adjust sandbox profile for required file system access

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Extension Error — Today Widget or Share Extension Not Working
item.description:
App Extension Error — Today Widget or Share Extension Not Working

Fix macOS app extension error: Today widget not loading, Share extension missing, app extension crashes, extension not appearing.

Common Causes

  • App extension not properly registered with system
  • Extension process crashed during initialization
  • Extension entitlements missing required capabilities
  • System UI cache preventing extensions from loading

How to Fix

1. Check App Extensions

# System Settings → Privacy & Security → Extensions → Review available extensions
# Or:pluginkit -m -v -i com.apple.appextension

2. Re-register Extensions

pluginkit -e reset
# Restart Mac to re-register all extensions

3. Reinstall App

# Delete and reinstall app to re-register its extensions

4. Check Extension Logs

log show --predicate 'process == "pluginkitd"' --last 1h | head -20

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Universal Binary Error — Not Optimized for Apple Silicon
item.description:
App Universal Binary Error — Not Optimized for Apple Silicon

Fix macOS Universal Binary error: app not optimized for Apple Silicon, universal binary showing Rosetta prompt, architecture mismatch.

Common Causes

  • App distributed as Intel-only binary without Apple Silicon support
  • Universal binary not properly built for both architectures
  • App installer placing wrong architecture version
  • Developer not providing Universal binary for the app

How to Fix

1. Check App Architecture

file /Applications/AppName.app/Contents/MacOS/AppName
# Look for 'Mach-O universal binary with 2 architectures' or 'x86_64' only

2. Run App in Rosetta

# Right-click app → Get Info → Open using Rosetta

3. Check for Apple Silicon Version

# Visit app developer website → Download Apple Silicon / Universal version

4. Contact Developer

# Ask developer to provide Apple Silicon native or Universal binary version

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Rosetta Error — Rosetta 2 Translation Failure
item.description:
App Rosetta Error — Rosetta 2 Translation Failure

Fix macOS Rosetta 2 error: Rosetta translation failure, app requires Rosetta, Rosetta not installed, translation layer error.

Common Causes

  • Rosetta 2 not installed on Apple Silicon Mac
  • App binary incompatible with Rosetta translation
  • Rosetta translation cache corrupted
  • App using system calls not supported by Rosetta

How to Fix

1. Install Rosetta 2

softwareupdate --install-rosetta
# Or: pkginstall /System/Library/CoreServices/RosettaUpdateAuto.pkg

2. Check Rosetta Status

arch -x86_64 /usr/bin/true && echo 'Rosetta installed' || echo 'Rosetta not installed'

3. Reset Rosetta Cache

sudo rm -rf /Library/Apple/usr/libexec/oah/*
# Reinstall Rosetta: softwareupdate --install-rosetta

4. Find Native Alternative

# Check if app has Apple Silicon native version available

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:

item.title: [Solution] macOS App Apple Silicon Error — ARM Binary Crash or Incompatibility
item.description:
App Apple Silicon Error — ARM Binary Crash or Incompatibility

Fix macOS Apple Silicon compatibility error: app not running on M1/M2/M3, ARM binary crash, architecture mismatch, silicon-specific error.

Common Causes

  • App built for wrong Apple Silicon architecture (M1 vs M2 vs M3)
  • App using deprecated system APIs removed in newer macOS
  • Apple Silicon-specific bug in app code
  • System extensions incompatible with Apple Silicon

How to Fix

1. Check App Compatibility

sw_vers
# Check Mac chip: system_profiler SPHardwareDataType | grep 'Chip'

2. Run in Rosetta

# Right-click app → Get Info → Open using Rosetta

3. Check for Updates

# App Store → Updates → Check for Apple Silicon compatible update

4. Contact Developer

# Report Apple Silicon compatibility issue to app developer

Common Scenarios

This error commonly occurs when:
